▾ ▸
Attributes
Body
i
Body
0
Precision
i
Precision
0
Quickness
i
Quickness
Quickness is your reflexes and your ability to react quickly to situations.
0
Conviction
i
Conviction
0
Intuition
i
Intuition
0
Reason
i
Reason
0
▾ ▸
Defense
Brace
i
Brace
Brace is your ability to resist a physical force by holding strong and blocking. Brace is calculated as: 7 + [Character Rank] + [Body]
0
Warding
i
Warding
Warding defense represents an attempt to disrupt the ether of a magical effect. Warding is calculated as: 7 + [Character Rank] + [Precision]
0
Evasion
i
Evasion
Evasion is your ability to complete evade an attack. Some especially slow attacks can be evaded and will check against evasion. Evasion is calculated as: 4 + [Character Rank] + [Quickness]
0
▾ ▸
Sense
Resolve
i
Resolve
Resolve is calculated as: 7 + [Character Rank] + [Conviction]
0
Ego
i
Ego
Ego is calculated as: 4 + [Character Rank] + [Intuition ]
0
Insight
i
Insight
Insight is calculated as: 7 + [Character Rank] + [Reason]
0
▾ ▸
General
Hit Points
i
Hit Points
Hit Points (HP) are the number of hits a character can take in combat. Your character’s hit points is a representation of your character maintaining their barrier to take hits, resisting harm with their toughness, and general ability to avoid harm. A character may be taking attacks from multiple sources that can be easily shrugged off, but once they run out of HP to do so is when the big hits make their way through. Hit Points is calculated as: 15 + [Character Rank x 10] + [Body]
0
Willpower
i
Willpower
Willpower is a character's ability to stay invested in a situation. Willpower is calculated as: 10 + [Character Rank x 10] + [Conviction]
0
Surge
i
Surge
Life Surge is a resource that allows a character to tap into their resolve to continue a fight. It is most often spent to restore their HP and Willpower. Whenever a will break is caused, a Surge is consumed and the character's Willpower restores to full. If you cannot consume a Surge, your Willpower remains at 0. After completing a short rest, 1 surge is restored. After completing a full rest, all surge is restored. Surge is calculated as: 3
0
EN
i
EN
EN is a resource that is spent when using more powerful techniques. Usually you will generate some at the start of every round in a combat. Some techniques can also generate EN. EN is calculated as: 9
0
Starting EN
i
Starting EN
This is how much EN you start with when finishing a rest. Starting EN is calculated as: 5
0
Round EN
i
Round EN
This is how much EN you gain at the start of a round during a conflict. Round EN is calculated as: 1
0
Recall
i
Recall
Recall is your ability to remember information learned in the past. It is used as a modifier when using Recall Knowledge to gain information. Recall is calculated as: [Character Rank] + [Reason]
0
Initiative
i
Initiative
The Initiative skill is used to determine whoever acts first in a conflict. Initiative is calculated as: [Character Rank] + [Quickness]
0
▾ ▸
Combat
Vitality
i
Vitality
Life Whenever you are reduced to zero HP, your HP restores to full and you lose one vitality. Unlike most resources, vitality can be reduced below 0. Whenever vitality is lost check if it is below 1. If it is, you gain the Downed status. If the character already had the Downed status, they also gain the Dying and Unconscious statuses which may ultimately kill them. Most characters will have a max vitality of 1. Player characters will usually start with 3 while special characters can have any amount. After completing a full rest, 1 vitality is healed. Vitality is calculated as: 1
0
Regen Value
i
Regen Value
Life This value is a standard amount of HP you recover from some healing abilities. Regen Value is calculated as: 15 + [Character Rank x 10] + [Conviction]
0
Armor
i
Armor
Life Armor reduces up to half of incoming HP damage from a single source by an amount equal to its rating. Armor is typically gained from gear, but techniques can grant armor temporarily. Armor is calculated as:
0
Burn Resist
i
Burn Resist
Life Resistance reduces up to half of incoming damage from a specific damage type by an amount equal to its rating. Burn Resist is calculated as:
0
Cold Resist
i
Cold Resist
Life Resistance reduces up to half of incoming damage from a specific damage type by an amount equal to its rating. Cold Resist is calculated as:
0
Energy Resist
i
Energy Resist
Life Resistance reduces up to half of incoming damage from a specific damage type by an amount equal to its rating. Energy Resist is calculated as:
0
Force Resist
i
Force Resist
Life Resistance reduces up to half of incoming damage from a specific damage type by an amount equal to its rating. Force Resist is calculated as:
0
Piercing Resist
i
Piercing Resist
Life Resistance reduces up to half of incoming damage from a specific damage type by an amount equal to its rating. Piercing Resist is calculated as:
0
Psyche Resist
i
Psyche Resist
Life Resistance reduces up to half of incoming damage from a specific damage type by an amount equal to its rating. Psyche Resist is calculated as:
0
Base Speed
i
Base Speed
Movement Base Speed is how much move charge a character gains at the start of a round. Base Speed is used to determine how fast the character moves out of combat. Base Speed is calculated as: 4
0
Dash Speed
i
Dash Speed
Movement Dash Speed is how much move charge a character gains when performing a dash. Dash Speed is used to determine how fast the character moves out of combat. Dash Speed is calculated as: 2
0
▾ ▸
Skills
Aesthetics
i
Aesthetics
Craft Aesthetics is the ability to design. This could be used to make an art piece more appealing or make a chair more comfortable to sit on.
0
Agility
i
Agility
Athletics Agility is an active skill used for feats of dexterity and reflex. This skill will most often find use whenever a technique requires swift or reflexive action such as quickly dodging an attack or keeping your balance.
0
Aim
i
Aim
Fight Aim is an active skill that allows one to pinpoint targets at a distance. It's commonly used with ranged weapon techniques and ranged spells.
0
Alchemy
i
Alchemy
Craft Alchemy is a technical skill used when making alchemical creations. These creations typically take the form of medicines, poisons, salves, oils, and other consumable crafts.
0
Analyze
i
Analyze
Perception Analyze is a technical skill used when information must be parsed quickly and efficiently. This skill is often used to find hidden clues that would not be in plain sight. It is also used whenever one wishes to quickly learn about a subject written in text or requires one to reason through a problem.
0
Build
i
Build
Craft Build is an active skill used whenever you are creating an object through precise technique. This skill is used mostly when you wish to create through the use of tools but some complicated items will require build to represent the precision required in the object being created.
0
Charm
i
Charm
Persuade Charm is a social skill used when attempting to meet another emotionally. This can be done through empathy, support, romance, and allure. Charm is used both for persuasion and deception, as long as the attempt is meant to appeal emotionally.
0
Cook
i
Cook
Craft Cook is a technical skill used when dealing with ingredients to be used for consumption and nutrition. This skill is not only used for traditional cooking but anytime food is prepared such as creating beverages. Food is an important resource to survive and keep spirits high.
0
Demoralize
i
Demoralize
Cunning Demoralize is a social skill associated with negativity and intimidating behaviour. Techniques that key off this skill usually attack willpower and invoke many negative emotions to control others' behaviour.
0
Energize
i
Energize
Cast You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill. Energize is a casting skill to create and maintain control over energies. It is most often used when concentrating on maintaining an energy you've created like fire, lighting, or cold.
0
Empathy
i
Empathy
Perception Empathy is a social skill used to actively detect another's emotional state. Empathy will often be used to sense another's convictions and influences. It can also see use when seeing through emotional deception.
0
Flux
i
Flux
Cast You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill. Flux is a casting skill used to create and manipulate forces. This is most often used to move objects but can also generate wind or apply forces to an area like a gravitational effect.
0
Glyphwork
i
Glyphwork
Device Glyphwork is a technical skill that allows one to create and manipulate pnevmarite technology. If a device requires magical tooling, this skill will often be used to work with it.
0
Grappling
i
Grappling
Fight Grappling is an active skill that represents attacking with one's own body. Wrestling and forcing movement are always grapple skill checks. In addition, many strikes from fists or kicks are often grappling checks.
0
Inspire
i
Inspire
Persuade Inspire is a social skill used to evoke feelings of hope and to counter dispair. Inspire is used when motivating action. Techniques that use inspire often provide emotional boosts and bolster willpower. Inspire is also often used in persuasion attempts to convince another to help.
0
Materialize
i
Materialize
Cast You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill. Materialize is a casting skill that creates matter by changing ambient ether into a desired form then stabilizes it. The material you create is determined by your elemental affinity(s). Wood creates pine, fire creates glass, earth creates granite, metal creates iron, and water creates ice.
0
Medicine
i
Medicine
Device Medicine is used when applying medical procedures like stitching a wound or using curatives.
0
Misdirect
i
Misdirect
Cunning Misdirect is a social skill used to obscure information through lies or omitting facts. Misdirect is most often applicable when using outright lies or tactics that try to redirect a conversation or one's attention.
0
Musicianship
i
Musicianship
Device Musicianship is the ability to perform with a musical instrument.
0
Notice
i
Notice
Perception Notice is used to find the hidden and anything that can be found with one's senses. Usually this is used to find characters hiding but can also be used to find hidden objects.
0
Physio
i
Physio
Cast You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill. Physio is a casting skill used to modify creatures and life. It is most often used for healing abilities but can also apply muscle stimulants to grant feats of physical strength.
0
Physique
i
Physique
Athletics Physique is an active skill used when strength is required to perform. This skill comes into play when a technique requires raw power such as shoving, jumping, climbing, or lifting.
0
Pilot
i
Pilot
Device Pilot is an active skill used to maneuver vehicles and machinery. Most complicated machinery will require a pilot check to utilize the device any many techniques with pilot allow you to perform complicated maneuvers with pilot.
0
Rationalize
i
Rationalize
Persuade Rationalize is a social skill used when persuading another through reason and logic. Rationalize is usually a tool for negotiation when appealing to a logical mindset such as when pragmatism is necessary. It can also be used to deceive using misinformation and selling it as truth.
0
Resonance
i
Resonance
Perception Resonance is a technical skill used to detect the presence of ether. This can be used to detect density of ether in an area but also can be used to determine if a material was made through ether. Complicated techniques can rely on resonance to remove the ether completely.
0
Skirmish
i
Skirmish
Fight Skirmish is an active skill for striking characters with melee attacks. It is exclusively an attacking skill for techniques that benefit from precise strikes to maximize effectiveness.
0
Tinker
i
Tinker
Device Tinker is a technical skill that allows one to create and manipulate machinery and technology. This skill can be used to disarm traps and locks. Otherwise, this will also be used when constructing complicated technology that require gears, pulleys, or other devices that require moving parts.
0
Transmute
i
Transmute
Cast You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill and available material to affect of which you have an elemental affinity it is composed of. This skill allows you to reshape the material into new forms like modifying terrain or changing the shape of dust into objects. This skill cannot modify material that is being resonated by another mage.
0
Actions
Gear
Forme
Character
Post
Details
Overview
?
Post
Overview
The Overview section shows quick information about your character. In addition, this is where you access both Advancement and Training to improve or change your character's build.
Details
The Details section contains all of your character's vital statistics. You can use this page to see exact numbers of each of their stats.
Post
This is the Post section. Here you can set your character emotes for the chat's messaging system. You can also use the Chat Post Box to send messages to the chat and select which language you are speaking from the language selection.
⟩⟩

Emotes
?
Outfits
Outfits
You can add in your character art from here to populate your character's emotes. Press the Plus (+) button below to add a new instance of an outfit. From there you can populate the outfit data with emotes.

?
Language Select
Language Select
Select your language from the options below. This will change how your message is displayed in chat and also show what language you are using.
?
Minere - Common
Minere
Walthair The common language used in Minerva and the lands surrounding it. Minere is made up of three root languages that were once spoken commonly amongst the coastal civilizations of the area.
?
Junal - Common
Junal
Aridsha Juno's major language and the official language of the Guidance. Those who live within or below the city of Juno are expected to learn and speak this language to fully integrate into society.
?
Apollen - Common
Apollen
Khem The common tongue of Apollo, this language uses a variety of symbols in written works. It is one of the oldest languages still in use in modern times. In the Blessed lands it is known as Mons, the language of the mountainous people, the Monsen.
?
Lib - Common
Lib
Colswei This language is commonly used in Liber. Its roots seem entirely locked to the region, however the age of the language itself are unclear due to it not being used in written form until much later than its equivalents.
?
Cert - Common
Cert
Ceres The most commonly spoken language in all of Ceres, this language is the original tongue used by the Choi clan. To most in modern times, this connection to the Choi clan is lost but it can often be made clear in the company of those of the clan.
?
Byric - Baryan Ascent
Byric
Aridsha The dialect of the tribes of people that live in the Baryan Ascent, north of the Aridsha Desert. It has many common traits with the neighboring dialect of Dustell.
?
Dustell - Aridsha
Dustell
Aridsha This language has many derrivatives across the tribes of the desert. However each version, while distinct in its pronunciations, share a common written form derrived from the Shira language. Due to its use throughout the desert, it is Juno's second most common language.
?
Muralic - Aridsha
Muralic
Aridsha The language of the Murali people in the deserts of Aridsha. Its roots in Shira are clear and may be even closer to the ancient language than Junal.
?
Shira - Ancient
Shira
Aridsha An old language that evolved into both the Junal and Muralic languages. Usage can still be found in ancient Guidance relics.
?
Ciel - Capitol
Ciel
Ceres This language is used almost exclusively by Clan Par, the historians of Ceres. Those who learn the language often do so to share in the clan's protection and love of the stories and values of Ceres.
?
Citeq - South West Ceres
Citeq
Ceres A spoken language used mostly by the Ceresian tribes that roam the south western plains of Ceres.
?
Manstan - Southern Ceres
Manstan
Ceres The spoken language used in Southern Ceresian tribes. It has mostly fallen out of favor due to many of the tribes in this location having settled into Sanctum life, however many who wish to remember older times still speak this language.
?
Salkan - North West Ceres
Salkan
Ceres An entirely spoken language used by the Ceresian tribes located in the North west Salkandu region. It is most commonly known as the language of Clan Han.
?
Sansic - Eastern Ceres
Sansic
Ceres The spoken language used in Eastern Ceresian tribes. It has mostly fallen out of favor due to many of the tribes in this location having settled into Sanctum life, however many who wish to remember older times still speak this language.
?
Silq - Western Ceres
Silq
Ceres The spoken language used in Western Ceresian forest tribes. This language is not often heard due to the forest peoples' exclusion from most of Ceres society in modern times.
?
Kleikan - Klef
Kleikan
Khem This spoken language was once the language of the Suntouched people that lived west of the Khembalung Mountain range. It's largely fallen out of favor but pockets of people still try to keep the language alive.
?
Crinere - Ancient
Crinere
Walthair The ancient language of the civilization of Metis. It has fallen out of favor for the modern Minere and is the basis for said language. Due to how similar the two languages are, many who speak Minere find themselves able to understand the scriptures of Crinere.
?
Palmic - Tropical Seas
Palmic
Walthair This spoken language is used exclusively in tropical island nations. In modern times it only sees common use by tribes in the region, however its language is often used in the tourism industry to imbue a sense of exoticism.
?
Shorespeak - East Sea
Shorespeak
Walthair A spoken language used by those living along the east coast and in island nations across the sea. While it has no written language, its similarities to both Minere and ancient Vulca make it easy to communicate across cultures regardless.
?
Verdeni - Verdant Key
Verdeni
Walthair The spoken language of the island of Verdant Key. Its peoples' isolation from most of modern society has kept this language in tact to ancient times.
?
Vulca - Ancient
Vulca
Walthair The ancient language of the civilization of Vulcan. It shares a lot of common traits of Shorespeak and may be its root language. The language has fallen out of favor for both Minere and Shorespeak but can still be found on ancient, Ocean Court relics and Vulcan texts.
?
Emotion - Spirit
Emotion
Special The language of the spirits. This language is incredibly simplistic as it is communicated entirely through emotions.
?
Empathy - Ancient Spirit
Empathy
Special A language of the spirits. This language is communicated through thought and ether, allowing creatures of any type to understand.
?
Wolfwarg - Cesplangrah
Wolfwarg
Special The spoken language of the bestial people, the Cesplangrah. Much of the language is spoken through grunts and growls.
?
Jovean - Ancient
Jovean
Special The common language of Novus, this language has been used across the blessed lands for as long as its people were aware. In the mainland, it is known as the language of the civilization of Jove and has largely fallen out of use.
?
Mytikan - Ancient
Mytikan
Special The root language of both Novan and Apollen, Mytikan is a language lost to time in the mainland. Ancient relics still use the language in the Blessed Lands and as such isn't surprising to find it still learned by scholars in Novus society.
▾ ▸

Forme
Character
?
Forme
Forme
All techniques are grouped into Styles. Broadly, a style is simply a group of techniques united by a theme. When entering a conflict, a character is limited in the number of styles they are able to set. When a style is set, the character has access to all techniques associated with that style. On this page you can set which styles are currently active on the character, allowing them to be used in the Actions Page. You can set both job styles and general styles. Basic styles are always set.
⟩⟩

▾ ▸
Currency
Jin
i
Jin
The currency of Minerva. This is paper money accepted throughout the continent of Wuxing due to Minerva's trade agreements throughout the land.
Gold
i
Gold
A rare resource known for its usages in magical material building. It was the preferred trading material until its use as a magical binding made it more scarse. Still, many small communities will use it for trade.
CP
i
CP
A coin currency that is popular in Apollo, Juno, and smaller civilizations. It is largely being replaced by the Minervan Jin due to the inconvenience of coins, but change at this scale is often difficult.

Actions
This is the Actions section. Here you can use any action available to your character.

Forme Techniques
Contents Hidden
Action
Range
EN
Will
Trigger.
Traits
Traits
 Traits .
Effects
Core Effects
 Effects
On Enter Effects
On Enter Effects
As long as this effect persists the effects below this entry all occur to any character in the area immediately on casting. These effects also occur to every character in the area at the start of every subsequent round - this happens after all sustained effects have been determined. These effects also occur for any character that enters the affected spaces. These effects can only trigger on a character once per round.
Skill Check Effects
Will Break Effects
Will Break Effects
 Will Break Effects
Increased Rank Effects
Increased Rank Effects
You may increase the rank of this technique so that it becomes more powerful. Each time you increase the rank you spend 1 additional EN to gain the effects listed below.
+ Increase Rank
+ Increase Rank
− Decrease Rank
− Decrease Rank
 
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide

On Enter Effects
As long as this effect persists the effects below this entry all occur to any character in the area immediately on casting. These effects also occur to every character in the area at the start of every subsequent round - this happens after all sustained effects have been determined. These effects also occur for any character that enters the affected spaces. These effects can only trigger on a character once per round.

On Enter Effects
On Enter Effects
As long as this effect persists the effects below this entry all occur to any character in the area immediately on casting. These effects also occur to every character in the area at the start of every subsequent round - this happens after all sustained effects have been determined. These effects also occur for any character that enters the affected spaces. These effects can only trigger on a character once per round.
Skill Check Effects
Will Break Effects
Will Break Effects
 Will Break Effects
Increased Rank Effects
Increased Rank Effects
You may increase the rank of this technique so that it becomes more powerful. Each time you increase the rank you spend 1 additional EN to gain the effects listed below.
+ Increase Rank
+ Increase Rank
− Decrease Rank
− Decrease Rank

On Enter Effects
On Enter Effects
As long as this effect persists the effects below this entry all occur to any character in the area immediately on casting. These effects also occur to every character in the area at the start of every subsequent round - this happens after all sustained effects have been determined. These effects also occur for any character that enters the affected spaces. These effects can only trigger on a character once per round.

Basic Techniques
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Basic Actions
Contents Hidden
Swift - 2/Round
1
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
Effects
You use your hand or hands to manipulate an object or the terrain. You can grab an unattended or stored object, open a door, or produce some similar effect. You might have to attempt a skill check to determine if your Interact action was successful.
Swift - 1/Turn
1-3
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
Effects
The target may use one Assist Technique.
Full - 1/Round
1-10
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
Effects
You allow another character to use a Quick-action Technique.
Full
Self
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. A range of Self means this technique can only target the user.
Traits. You must consume a Boon to use this technique.
Effects
You may change your equipped Job and Styles.
 
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Detection and Hiding
Contents Hidden
Quick
Self
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. A range of Self means this technique can only target the user.
Effects
Core Effects
[Hidden]
You can’t be targeted by hostile attacks or actions, don’t cause engagement, and enemies only know your approximate location. Attacking, taking reactions, moving, and losing cover all remove this condition after they resolve. Characters can find you with Search. To remain hidden you must either be outside of any enemies’ line of sight, obscured by sufficient cover, or invisible. If you Hide while meeting one of these criteria, you gain the Hidden status. Hard cover is sufficient to Hide as long as it is large enough to totally conceal you, but soft cover is only sufficient if you are completely inside an area or zone that grants soft cover.
You gain the Hidden Status
Quick
1
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Cone X
This technique affects all characters within a cone, X spaces long and X spaces wide at its furthest point. The cone begins at one space wide and increases the width by 1 as distance increases.
Cone 5
You detect the presence of ether.
DC 10 Resonance
Skill Check Effects
[DC X Skill Check]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this value is equal to or greater than X you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.
You can confirm if ether is particularly dense in your selected area and if any elemental affinity is present. Usually this will detect living creatures or active magical effects in the area but not specifically where it is. Rarely, this can detect strong ether lingering from the environment itself.
Quick
1-4
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
Traits. Target must be a character you suspect is Hidden.
You try to find a hidden character.
Notice vs. Evasion
Skill Check Effects
[Skill Check vs. X]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this total value is equal to or greater than the target's X defense you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Hidden]
You can’t be targeted by hostile attacks or actions, don’t cause engagement, and enemies only know your approximate location. Attacking, taking reactions, moving, and losing cover all remove this condition after they resolve. Characters can find you with Search. To remain hidden you must either be outside of any enemies’ line of sight, obscured by sufficient cover, or invisible. If you Hide while meeting one of these criteria, you gain the Hidden status. Hard cover is sufficient to Hide as long as it is large enough to totally conceal you, but soft cover is only sufficient if you are completely inside an area or zone that grants soft cover.
Target loses the Hidden Status
 
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Spirit Actions
Contents Hidden
Full
1
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
Traits. You must share a space with a target creature in the material plane. The target must not have an elemental aspect.
You manifest into the material plane by entering a creature.
Effects
Core Effects
[Ethereal]
You are in the spirit realm. If you have a physical body it is treated as unconscious. Nothing in the physical plane may interact with your ethereal form and likewise, you may not interact with characters or objects in the physical plane.
You lose the Ethereal Status The spirit is considered Manifesting into a host. A spirit manifesting into a creature gains the senses of the creature and can communicate with their host through thoughts as long as both share a language. . While Manifesting in a creature, all resource costs for your techniques are consumed by your host instead of yourself.
Swift
Self
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. A range of Self means this technique can only target the user.
Traits. You must be manifesting in a host.
Effects
Core Effects
[Ethereal]
You are in the spirit realm. If you have a physical body it is treated as unconscious. Nothing in the physical plane may interact with your ethereal form and likewise, you may not interact with characters or objects in the physical plane.
You gain the Ethereal Status You leave your host and enter the spirit plane in the same space.
Passive
0
Traits. You must be manifesting in a creature that can cast magic.
Effects
Creatures you are manifesting within may equip techniques as if they have your affinity.
 
▾ ▸
Combat Techniques
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Basic Attacks
Contents Hidden
Assist
1
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
Traits. Target must be the same Size or smaller than you.
Effects
Core Effects
[EN]
EN is a resource that is spent when using more powerful techniques. Usually you will generate some at the start of every round in a combat. Some techniques can also generate EN.
You gain 1 EN
Skirmish vs. Brace
Skill Check Effects
[Skill Check vs. X]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this total value is equal to or greater than the target's X defense you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Damage]
This technique deals damage to the target's HP or Willpower, decreasing their current value. If you fail to pass the skill check versus the target's defense, you then check if you pass against their evasion defense. If you pass you still deal half damage.

[Move]
When a character moves, that character can move into any adjacent space, as long as the space isn’t occupied by an obstruction and is one that they would be able to move in. For example, characters can't move straight up unless they can fly.
Each space you move consumes 1 space of your movement on conpletion of the move. When moving diagonally, you must consume 1 additional space of movement upon entering the space. You are unable to move any more spaces once you are at 0 movement.
Sometimes a character will be required to swim to enter a large body of liquid or climb when moving across a terrain vertically. These are special forms of movement done as part of any Move action.
When swimming or climbing you must consume 1 additional space of movement upon entering the space. A successful DC 10 Physique skill check may be required before entering a space if it is especially difficult to swim or climb.
Target takes 1d3 + [Potency] Force damage Target is Pushed 2 spaces from you
Increased Rank Effects
Increased Rank Effects
You may increase the rank of this technique so that it becomes more powerful. Each time you increase the rank you spend 1 additional EN to gain the effects listed below.
Increase Force damage by 2d3
Assist
1
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
You swing a weapon at a target.
Effects
Core Effects
[EN]
EN is a resource that is spent when using more powerful techniques. Usually you will generate some at the start of every round in a combat. Some techniques can also generate EN.
You gain 1 EN
Skirmish vs. Brace
Skill Check Effects
[Skill Check vs. X]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this total value is equal to or greater than the target's X defense you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Damage]
This technique deals damage to the target's HP or Willpower, decreasing their current value. If you fail to pass the skill check versus the target's defense, you then check if you pass against their evasion defense. If you pass you still deal half damage.
Target takes 3d3 + [Potency] Weapon damage
Increased Rank Effects
Increased Rank Effects
You may increase the rank of this technique so that it becomes more powerful. Each time you increase the rank you spend 1 additional EN to gain the effects listed below.
Increase Weapon damage by 2d3
Assist
2-4
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.
Range values that don't include 1 in their range are not allowed to be used in melee range. If a target is directly adjacent to the user the target is invalid.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
Traits
Traits
[Bulk 15[MAX]]
The object must be of or carrying Bulk 15 or less.
An object of Bulk 15 is typically something up to 20 lbs and can easily be carried in both of a person's hands. Examples include large swords, boxes (1.5 feet cubed), a small creature, or many armor pieces.
. You must equip gear with the trait Bulk 15[MAX].
You throw an object at a target.
Effects
Core Effects
[EN]
EN is a resource that is spent when using more powerful techniques. Usually you will generate some at the start of every round in a combat. Some techniques can also generate EN.
You gain 1 EN
Aim vs. Brace
Skill Check Effects
[Skill Check vs. X]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this total value is equal to or greater than the target's X defense you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Damage]
This technique deals damage to the target's HP or Willpower, decreasing their current value. If you fail to pass the skill check versus the target's defense, you then check if you pass against their evasion defense. If you pass you still deal half damage.
Target takes 2d3 + [Potency] Weapon damage
Increased Rank Effects
Increased Rank Effects
You may increase the rank of this technique so that it becomes more powerful. Each time you increase the rank you spend 1 additional EN to gain the effects listed below.
Increase Weapon damage by 2d3

Basic Movement
Contents Hidden
Quick - 3/Round
Self
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. A range of Self means this technique can only target the user.
Effects
Core Effects
[Move]
When a character moves, that character can move into any adjacent space, as long as the space isn’t occupied by an obstruction and is one that they would be able to move in. For example, characters can't move straight up unless they can fly.
Each space you move consumes 1 space of your movement on conpletion of the move. When moving diagonally, you must consume 1 additional space of movement upon entering the space. You are unable to move any more spaces once you are at 0 movement.
Sometimes a character will be required to swim to enter a large body of liquid or climb when moving across a terrain vertically. These are special forms of movement done as part of any Move action.
When swimming or climbing you must consume 1 additional space of movement upon entering the space. A successful DC 10 Physique skill check may be required before entering a space if it is especially difficult to swim or climb.
You may Move up to [Dash Speed] spaces
Quick
Self
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. A range of Self means this technique can only target the user.
Effects
Core Effects
[Move]
When a character moves, that character can move into any adjacent space, as long as the space isn’t occupied by an obstruction and is one that they would be able to move in. For example, characters can't move straight up unless they can fly.
Each space you move consumes 1 space of your movement on conpletion of the move. When moving diagonally, you must consume 1 additional space of movement upon entering the space. You are unable to move any more spaces once you are at 0 movement.
Sometimes a character will be required to swim to enter a large body of liquid or climb when moving across a terrain vertically. These are special forms of movement done as part of any Move action.
When swimming or climbing you must consume 1 additional space of movement upon entering the space. A successful DC 10 Physique skill check may be required before entering a space if it is especially difficult to swim or climb.
You jumps 2 spaces wide
DC 10 Physique
Skill Check Effects
[DC X Skill Check]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this value is equal to or greater than X you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Move]
When a character moves, that character can move into any adjacent space, as long as the space isn’t occupied by an obstruction and is one that they would be able to move in. For example, characters can't move straight up unless they can fly.
Each space you move consumes 1 space of your movement on conpletion of the move. When moving diagonally, you must consume 1 additional space of movement upon entering the space. You are unable to move any more spaces once you are at 0 movement.
Sometimes a character will be required to swim to enter a large body of liquid or climb when moving across a terrain vertically. These are special forms of movement done as part of any Move action.
When swimming or climbing you must consume 1 additional space of movement upon entering the space. A successful DC 10 Physique skill check may be required before entering a space if it is especially difficult to swim or climb.
You jumps 1 spaces high and 1 spaces wide
Quick
Self
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. A range of Self means this technique can only target the user.
Effects
Core Effects
[Move]
When a character moves, that character can move into any adjacent space, as long as the space isn’t occupied by an obstruction and is one that they would be able to move in. For example, characters can't move straight up unless they can fly.
Each space you move consumes 1 space of your movement on conpletion of the move. When moving diagonally, you must consume 1 additional space of movement upon entering the space. You are unable to move any more spaces once you are at 0 movement.
Sometimes a character will be required to swim to enter a large body of liquid or climb when moving across a terrain vertically. These are special forms of movement done as part of any Move action.
When swimming or climbing you must consume 1 additional space of movement upon entering the space. A successful DC 10 Physique skill check may be required before entering a space if it is especially difficult to swim or climb.
You may Move up to 1 spaces. This movement does not break the hidden or invisible condition
DC 10 Agility
Skill Check Effects
[DC X Skill Check]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this value is equal to or greater than X you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Move]
When a character moves, that character can move into any adjacent space, as long as the space isn’t occupied by an obstruction and is one that they would be able to move in. For example, characters can't move straight up unless they can fly.
Each space you move consumes 1 space of your movement on conpletion of the move. When moving diagonally, you must consume 1 additional space of movement upon entering the space. You are unable to move any more spaces once you are at 0 movement.
Sometimes a character will be required to swim to enter a large body of liquid or climb when moving across a terrain vertically. These are special forms of movement done as part of any Move action.
When swimming or climbing you must consume 1 additional space of movement upon entering the space. A successful DC 10 Physique skill check may be required before entering a space if it is especially difficult to swim or climb.
You may Move up to [Dash Speed] spaces. This movement does not break the hidden or invisible condition
 
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Basic Recovery
Contents Hidden
Quick
1
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
Traits. You have the grappled status.
You attempt to end a grapple or restraining effect by breaking the grappler's grip.
Any Athletics vs. Brace
Skill Check Effects
[Skill Check vs. X]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this total value is equal to or greater than the target's X defense you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Grappled]
You cannot make any voluntary movements, although involuntary movements are unaffected. You move when the controlling character moves, mirroring their movement. A grapple automatically ends when either character breaks adjacency, such as if they are knocked back by another effect or if the controller chooses to end the grapple as a reaction.
You lose the Grappled Status
Quick
Self
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. A range of Self means this technique can only target the user.
4 EN
You calm your emotions to refocus.
Effects
Core Effects
[]
You lose all emotions of your choice
Full
1
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target or Self
A range of Target/Self has a specified target character or object for the technique that may also be used to target the user. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target/Self
You try to remove a condition afffecting you or an ally.
Effects
Core Effects
[]
Target loses any condition of your choice
Full
Self
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. A range of Self means this technique can only target the user.
You take a moment to address your wounds.
Effects
Core Effects
[]
If You has a surge, they must spend one and heal [Regen Value] Hit Points You lose any condition of your choice
Reaction - 1/Round
Self
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. A range of Self means this technique can only target the user.
Trigger. When you fall or pass an edge or other handhold
DC 10 Any Athletics
Skill Check Effects
[DC X Skill Check]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this value is equal to or greater than X you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.
You grab the ledge, potentially stopping your fall.
 
▾ ▸
Social Techniques
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Agitation
Contents Hidden
Quick
1-10
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
Traits
Traits
[]
. This is a Social technique.
Your words are designed to frustrate
Effects
Core Effects
[Impatience]
Impatience measures a character's willingness to continue speaking on a subject. Once Impatience reaches 8, a character has a chance to end their involvement in a diplomatic request. And once it reaches 18 they may end their invovlement at their own will.
Target gains 1 Impatience
Any Cunning vs. Insight
Skill Check Effects
[Skill Check vs. X]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this total value is equal to or greater than the target's X defense you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Damage]
This technique deals damage to the target's HP or Willpower, decreasing their current value. If you fail to pass the skill check versus the target's defense, you then check if you pass against their ego defense. If you pass you still deal half damage.

[Distracted]
You gain Disadvantage on all skill checks per rank of the Distracted emotion.
This Emotion ends when it is triggered or at the start of the next round.
Target takes 1d3 + [Potency] Will damage Target gains 1 ranks in the Distracted Status
Will Break Effects
Will Break Effects
Will Break
When a character's Willpower depletes to zero they can suffer from a technique's will break effects. You can choose to not trigger a technique's will break effects. When a will break is triggered, the effects listed occur to the target and then their Willpower restores to full.

[Flustered]
All skill checks against a flustered target receive +1 Advantage and request checks against the target increase by 5.
Target gains the Flustered Status
Increased Rank Effects
Increased Rank Effects
You may increase the rank of this technique so that it becomes more powerful. Each time you increase the rank you spend 1 additional EN to gain the effects listed below.
Increase Will damage by 2d3
Quick
1-10
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
Traits
Traits
[]
. This is a Social technique.
Your words are designed to anger
Effects
Core Effects
[Impatience]
Impatience measures a character's willingness to continue speaking on a subject. Once Impatience reaches 8, a character has a chance to end their involvement in a diplomatic request. And once it reaches 18 they may end their invovlement at their own will.
Target gains 1 Impatience
Any Cunning vs. Resolve
Skill Check Effects
[Skill Check vs. X]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this total value is equal to or greater than the target's X defense you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Damage]
This technique deals damage to the target's HP or Willpower, decreasing their current value. If you fail to pass the skill check versus the target's defense, you then check if you pass against their ego defense. If you pass you still deal half damage.

[Distracted]
You gain Disadvantage on all skill checks per rank of the Distracted emotion.
This Emotion ends when it is triggered or at the start of the next round.
Target takes 1d3 + [Potency] Will damage Target gains 1 ranks in the Distracted Status
Will Break Effects
Will Break Effects
Will Break
When a character's Willpower depletes to zero they can suffer from a technique's will break effects. You can choose to not trigger a technique's will break effects. When a will break is triggered, the effects listed occur to the target and then their Willpower restores to full.

[Angered]
When you use an [Attack] or [Social] technique and it does not include the source of your anger, you lose (5 x Character Rank) Willpower and 5 + (5 x Character Rank) HP. This cannot cause a Will Break.. An Angered character can only have one character be the target of this emotion and will have the target replaced by a new target if this emotion is gained again. If you are calmed, the calmed emotion ends.
Target gains the Angered Status
Increased Rank Effects
Increased Rank Effects
You may increase the rank of this technique so that it becomes more powerful. Each time you increase the rank you spend 1 additional EN to gain the effects listed below.
Increase Will damage by 2d3
 
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Favor and Influence
Contents Hidden
Full
1-10
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
Traits
Traits
[]
. This is a Social technique.
You attempt to make a good impression with a specific person.
Effects
Core Effects
[Impatience]
Impatience measures a character's willingness to continue speaking on a subject. Once Impatience reaches 8, a character has a chance to end their involvement in a diplomatic request. And once it reaches 18 they may end their invovlement at their own will.
Target gains 2 Impatience
Any Persuade vs. Ego
Skill Check Effects
[Skill Check vs. X]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this total value is equal to or greater than the target's X defense you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Favor]
Favor is a measure of how much a character is agreeable to you during a diplomatic request. Favor is most often used as a bonus when making a request check.
There is a cap to the amount of favor that can be added during a request check. This cap is equal to half of the DC of your request.
Target gains 1d3 + 2 Favor
Quick - 1/Round
1-10
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
1 EN
Traits
Traits
[]
. This is a Social technique. Choose a recent topic or situation where you've seen the reaction of the target.
You try to gauge how a recent action, something said, or other situation impacted them.
Empathy vs. Insight
Skill Check Effects
[Skill Check vs. X]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this total value is equal to or greater than the target's X defense you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Influence]
An influence is a phrase or desription of something of some importance to the character. This technique will modify an influence so that for the duration of the combat that influence's rank will increase or decrease.
In Diplomatic Request Combat, you will be making statements as part of your social techniques. If you say something that can affect an influence that may either make the target agreeable or oppositional with a number of ranks equal to the influence's value.
When using Social techniques against an agreeable target, you add advantage to your skill check equal to the ranks. An oppositional target adds disadvantage to these ranks.
When performing a request, you add 5 x the rank of agreeable to the request check, or remove the same amount when oppositional.
A related influence to the statement is revealed to you. You learn whether the influence is supportive or oppositional
Full
1-10
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
1 EN
Traits
Traits
[]
. This is a Social technique. The target must have an influence you can leverage to support your check. This influence must also be of the severity you wish to change an influence to.
You try to convince another that a held conviction of theirs is more or less important.
Effects
Core Effects
[Impatience]
Impatience measures a character's willingness to continue speaking on a subject. Once Impatience reaches 8, a character has a chance to end their involvement in a diplomatic request. And once it reaches 18 they may end their invovlement at their own will.
Target gains 2 Impatience
Any Persuade vs. Resolve
Skill Check Effects
[Skill Check vs. X]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this total value is equal to or greater than the target's X defense you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Damage]
This technique deals damage to the target's HP or Willpower, decreasing their current value. If you fail to pass the skill check versus the target's defense, you then check if you pass against their ego defense. If you pass you still deal half damage.
Target takes 8d3 + [Potency x 2] Will damage
Will Break Effects
Will Break Effects
Will Break
When a character's Willpower depletes to zero they can suffer from a technique's will break effects. You can choose to not trigger a technique's will break effects. When a will break is triggered, the effects listed occur to the target and then their Willpower restores to full.

[Influence]
An influence is a phrase or desription of something of some importance to the character. This technique will modify an influence so that for the duration of the combat that influence's rank will increase or decrease.
In Diplomatic Request Combat, you will be making statements as part of your social techniques. If you say something that can affect an influence that may either make the target agreeable or oppositional with a number of ranks equal to the influence's value.
When using Social techniques against an agreeable target, you add advantage to your skill check equal to the ranks. An oppositional target adds disadvantage to these ranks.
When performing a request, you add 5 x the rank of agreeable to the request check, or remove the same amount when oppositional.
Raise or lower the severity of an influence on your target
Quick
Self
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. A range of Self means this technique can only target the user.
4 EN
You calm your emotions to refocus.
Effects
Core Effects
[]
You lose all emotions of your choice
Full
1
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target or Self
A range of Target/Self has a specified target character or object for the technique that may also be used to target the user. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target/Self
You try to remove a condition afffecting you or an ally.
Effects
Core Effects
[]
Target loses any condition of your choice
 
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Requests
Contents Hidden
Full
1-10
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. The numbers specified indicate how many spaces away from the user the target may be to be a valid target.

Target
A range of Target has a single specified target character or object for the technique. This technique may have special qualities that also affect the user.
One Target
Traits
Traits
[]
. This is a Social technique.
You make a request of someone, leveraging the favor you've built.
Effects
Core Effects
[Impatience]
Impatience measures a character's willingness to continue speaking on a subject. Once Impatience reaches 8, a character has a chance to end their involvement in a diplomatic request. And once it reaches 18 they may end their invovlement at their own will.

[Request Check]
When making a request check restate what you want from this request. The nature of the request will set the difficulty class (DC) of the request check.
The request check then gets calculated. When adding favor you are capped to half of the DC of the request.
In addition to the values displayed here, you will also add 5 x the target's agreeable rank and you will subtract 5 x the target's oppositional rank. If the target has the Flustered condition you will also add 5 to your check.
Request DCs

[+1 Low]
A low severity influence is usually notable in the short term and not important to who that character is. These influences can be long term goals but are also often fleeting desires from the moment they are formed.

[+2 Moderate]
A moderate severity influence are typically formed long term and have a hold on the character's convictions and desires. These influences can sway decisions even when tangentially related to a situation. Moderate influences often dicate how the character lives their daily lives. These influences can usually be leveraged to persuade a character to work outside of their self interest and stick their neck out for a greater cause.

[+3 High]
High severity influences are the most important beliefs, relationships, and goals of a character. These define who they are as a person, often guiding their behavior in all aspects of their lives. They can persuade a character to take great action that may even alter their own lives.
When making your request, you may attempt to bargain at the same time by offering goods, services, or favors. You may also choose to threaten and offering them things they do not want at the risk of angering them. This may increase the results of your request check. Target gains 2 Impatience Make a request check on the target with [Target's Favor]
Any Persuade vs. Ego
Skill Check Effects
[Skill Check vs. X]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this total value is equal to or greater than the target's X defense you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Request Check]
When making a request check restate what you want from this request. The nature of the request will set the difficulty class (DC) of the request check.
The request check then gets calculated. When adding favor you are capped to half of the DC of the request.
In addition to the values displayed here, you will also add 5 x the target's agreeable rank and you will subtract 5 x the target's oppositional rank. If the target has the Flustered condition you will also add 5 to your check.
Request DCs

[+1 Low]
A low severity influence is usually notable in the short term and not important to who that character is. These influences can be long term goals but are also often fleeting desires from the moment they are formed.

[+2 Moderate]
A moderate severity influence are typically formed long term and have a hold on the character's convictions and desires. These influences can sway decisions even when tangentially related to a situation. Moderate influences often dicate how the character lives their daily lives. These influences can usually be leveraged to persuade a character to work outside of their self interest and stick their neck out for a greater cause.

[+3 High]
High severity influences are the most important beliefs, relationships, and goals of a character. These define who they are as a person, often guiding their behavior in all aspects of their lives. They can persuade a character to take great action that may even alter their own lives.
Make a request check on the target with 1d3
Quick - 1/Turn
Self
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. A range of Self means this technique can only target the user.
Traits
Traits
[]
. This is a Social technique. You must have at least 8 Impatience.
Effects
Add your total Impatience to the skill check.
DC 18 Conviction
Skill Check Effects
[DC X Skill Check]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this value is equal to or greater than X you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.
You remove yourself from the conversation and can no longer be requested of anything.
Full - 1/Round
Self
Range
This value represents the range and targetting pattern for this technique. A range of Self means this technique can only target the user.
Traits

[]
. This is a Social technique.
Effects
Core Effects
[Impatience]
Impatience measures a character's willingness to continue speaking on a subject. Once Impatience reaches 8, a character has a chance to end their involvement in a diplomatic request. And once it reaches 18 they may end their invovlement at their own will.
You gain 2 Impatience
Quick - 1/Turn

[]
. This is a Social technique.
DC 10 Intuition
Skill Check Effects
[DC X Skill Check]
When you use this technique make a skill check by rolling 2d6 and adding it to your appropriate skill's value. If this value is equal to or greater than X you pass the check and all of the effects listed in this block occur.

[Impatience]
Impatience measures a character's willingness to continue speaking on a subject. Once Impatience reaches 8, a character has a chance to end their involvement in a diplomatic request. And once it reaches 18 they may end their invovlement at their own will.
You gain 1 Impatience

Effects
Core Effects
 Effects
On Enter Effects
On Enter Effects
As long as this effect persists the effects below this entry all occur to any character in the area immediately on casting. These effects also occur to every character in the area at the start of every subsequent round - this happens after all sustained effects have been determined. These effects also occur for any character that enters the affected spaces. These effects can only trigger on a character once per round.

Increased Rank Effects
Increased Rank Effects
You may increase the rank of this technique so that it becomes more powerful. Each time you increase the rank you spend 1 additional EN to gain the effects listed below.
+ Increase Rank
+ Increase Rank
